originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: A President Wins Big in Djibouti!
content:
Hello from Djibouti!

Welcome, kids! Today we zoom to a special country in Africa. It's called Djibouti! Say it with me: Ji-BOO-tee. It's a small country in East Africa, right by the big blue sea. Djibouti is a very hot place, with lots of sunshine every day. It has beautiful beaches and some amazing salt lakes! Did you know? Djibouti is famous for its bright blue waters where you can see amazing fish like the whale shark!

What is an Election?

Imagine your classroom. Sometimes you might choose a class monitor, right? Or maybe you choose a captain for your sports team. That's a bit like an election! It's when grown-ups in a country get to choose their leaders. They vote for the person they think will do the best job for everyone. Every vote is important! It helps decide who becomes the leader for the whole country. This is how countries decide who will help guide them. Did you know? In some countries, people mark their choice on a piece of paper, and in others, they use special machines to vote!

Meet President Ismail!

In Djibouti, the grown-ups just had an election. They chose their president again! His name is Ismail Omar Guelleh. That's a long name! Let's call him President Ismail for short. President Ismail is 78 years old. That's older than most of your grandparents! He has been the president of Djibouti for a very, very long time. He first became president 27 years ago. Imagine all the things that have happened in 27 years! He has led his country through many changes. Did you know? A president is like the chief helper for the whole country! They make important decisions about schools, roads, and keeping people safe.

A Big Win for President Ismail

In this latest election, President Ismail won by a huge number of votes. Almost everyone who voted chose him! He got nearly 98 out of every 100 votes. Wow! That's a very big win, just like if almost all your friends wanted to play the same game. This means he will continue to be the president of Djibouti. This is the sixth time he has won! That's a lot of wins for one person. He will keep working for the people of Djibouti. He will continue to guide his country for another few years. Did you know? Winning an election with many votes means the people trust you a lot to lead them and make good choices.

What About Other Candidates?

Sometimes, in elections, there are many people who want to be president. These people are called candidates. They all hope to win votes. But in this election in Djibouti, some other grown-ups who wanted to be president decided not to take part. They stayed home instead of asking people to vote for them. So, President Ismail was the only grown-up asking for votes from all the people. This made it easier for him to win by a very large number. It's like playing a game where only one person is trying to score points. They are sure to get many points! Did you know? Having many different candidates means people have lots of choices for who they want as a leader! This is called a choice.

Why Are Leaders Important?

Just like your parents help you, or your teacher helps your class, a president helps their whole country. Leaders make decisions about schools, hospitals, and keeping people safe. They work hard to make life better for everyone in their country. Good leaders listen to their people and try their best to solve problems. In Djibouti, President Ismail will keep guiding his country. He will help Djibouti grow and stay a peaceful place for everyone to live. Did you know? Africa has 54 countries, and each one has its own special leader! Each leader works for their own country.

Life in Djibouti

Djibouti is a really interesting place! Even though it's hot, many people enjoy life there. They love to drink tea and coffee. Some people enjoy fishing in the clear blue sea. You might even see some camels walking around in the desert areas! The capital city is also called Djibouti City. It's a busy place with a port. A port is where big ships come and go, bringing things to the country and taking things away. Did you know? Djibouti is home to an animal called the Dibatag, which is a type of antelope with a very long neck! It's super cool.

Learning About Our World

It's fun to learn about different countries in Africa and around the world. Every country has its own special stories and its own leaders. We can always learn something new by listening to the news. So, next time you hear about a country, maybe you can find it on a map and learn more! That's all the news from Djibouti for today, little explorers! Keep being curious about our amazing world and all the people in it! Did you know? Knowing about leaders helps us understand how countries work together and make friends across the globe!
summary: In the African country of Djibouti, a grown-up named President Ismail Omar Guelleh won an election with almost all the votes, and he will be president for a very long time, for his sixth time.
whyMatters: It's important to know about leaders in different countries because they help guide their people and make big decisions, just like your teacher helps guide your class every day.

originalTitle: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
title: Good News for Kids: Making Nigeria a Safer Place!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, super friends from all over Africa and beyond! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share some important and good news with you. We're talking about a very big and beautiful country in Africa called Nigeria. Nigeria is a wonderful place with lots of different people, amazing music, and yummy food! But sometimes, like in any place, there can be a little bit of trouble. Today's news is about how brave grown-ups are working to make sure Nigeria is a super safe and happy place for all its children.

What Happened in Nigeria?

Imagine you have a playground. Most kids play nicely, right? But what if a few kids were always pushing and being mean? Well, something a bit like that was happening in parts of Nigeria. There were some groups of grown-ups who were making trouble. These grown-ups were part of groups that hurt people and stopped them from living peacefully. They were called “militant Islamists” – but that's a big grown-up word. Think of them as people who were not playing by the rules and were causing problems for others.

Brave Judges Made Big Decisions!

In Nigeria, there are very important grown-ups called judges. Judges are like the super-referees for fairness and rules in a country. When someone does something wrong, the judges listen very carefully to all the stories. They try to find out what really happened. They want to make sure everyone is treated fairly, but also that people who break the rules learn from their mistakes. These judges had many, many meetings, called “trials,” to talk about the grown-ups who were making trouble.

Who Were the Troublemakers?

Nearly 400 grown-ups were involved in these troubles. They were part of groups that did not want peace. They caused fear and sadness in some communities. Nobody wants to feel scared or sad, especially not children! These groups sometimes tricked people or forced them to do bad things. But it's important to remember that most people in Nigeria, and most people who are Muslims, are good and kind. These troublemakers were just a very small part of many people.

Did you know? Nigeria has the most people of any country in Africa! It has over 200 million people, like a giant, busy family!

What Does "Sentenced" Mean?

After listening to everything, the judges made a big decision. This is called “sentencing.” When someone is sentenced, it means the judge decides what should happen to them because they broke the rules. It's like a time-out, but a very serious one. For these grown-ups who caused trouble, the judges decided they needed to go away for a while. Some of them will stay in a special place for five years. That's a long time – maybe from when you are little until you are much bigger!

Learning to Be Better

Other grown-ups who caused even bigger problems will stay in that special place for a very, very long time. We call this “life imprisonment.” It means they will stay there for the rest of their lives. This is to make sure they can no longer hurt anyone. This isn't about being mean. It's about giving these people a chance to think about what they did. It's also to keep everyone else safe. When bad things happen, judges help to make sure that people learn to be better and that good people are protected.

Making Nigeria Safe for YOU!

So, why is this important news for you, our super kid friends? Because it means that grown-ups in Nigeria are working very hard to make sure you can grow up safe and happy. They want you to be able to play, go to school, and dream big dreams without any fear. When judges make decisions like this, it helps to bring peace. It shows everyone that if you cause trouble and hurt others, there will be consequences. This helps make sure everyone follows the rules and treats each other with kindness.

Did you know? The capital city of Nigeria is Abuja. It's a very modern and important city!

Dreams for a Peaceful Future

Imagine a Nigeria where every child can laugh loudly, run freely, and learn new things every day. That's what these brave judges and many other good people are working towards. They want a future where kindness wins and everyone can live together happily. We all have a part to play in this! We can be kind to our friends, share our toys, and help others. When we do good things, we help build a better, more peaceful world for everyone.

Did you know? Nigeria is famous for its amazing music, especially a style called Afrobeats! Maybe you've danced to some Nigerian songs without even knowing it!

Let's Keep Hope Alive!

This news shows that even when there's trouble, there are always good people working to make things right. It's a message of hope and strength for Nigeria. We hope that these decisions will help bring even more peace and smiles to all the children of Nigeria. Remember, you are important, and your safety matters! Keep shining bright, little stars! Until next time, this is your friend reporter, signing off with a big wave!
summary: Judges in Nigeria have made important decisions to help stop people who cause trouble, making the country safer for children.
whyMatters: This is important because it means grown-ups are working hard to keep everyone, especially kids, safe and happy in Nigeria, so they can play and learn without fear.

originalTitle: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
title: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
content: UK government officials say they are not entirely abandoning the agreement but have run out of time.

originalTitle: Dozens feared dead in air strike on village in northeastern Nigeria
title: Sad News from Jilli Village: Let's Talk About Safety and Kindness
content:
Hello my wonderful friends! I'm your friendly news reporter for Africa. Today, I have some sad news to share from a place called Jilli village, which is in a big country named Nigeria. Nigeria is right here in our amazing continent of Africa.

What Happened in Jilli Village?

Something very sad happened in Jilli village. Imagine a busy, happy market, full of people laughing, talking, and buying yummy food or bright clothes. Markets are super important places where people meet their friends, sell things they've made, and buy things they need for their homes. But one day, a very sad thing happened there. A military plane, which is like a big airplane that people who protect the country use, accidentally dropped something. This caused a big problem at the market.

A Very Sad Day

Many, many people were at the market that day. They were just doing their shopping, selling their produce, and chatting with friends, just like we might do at a shop or a school fair. When the sad thing happened, lots of people got hurt. It was a very, very sad day for Jilli village and all the families who live there. Some grown-ups say that more than 100 people were hurt or lost their lives. That's a lot of people, like all the children in many classrooms put together. It makes us feel very sad when we hear about people getting hurt, especially in a place where they should feel safe, like a market.

What is an 'Air Strike'?

When we talk about an 'air strike,' it means that something was dropped from an airplane in the sky. Usually, this is done by the military. The military is a group of brave people in uniforms who work to protect their country and keep everyone safe. But sometimes, even brave people can make mistakes, or accidents can happen, and that's what we think might have happened here. It was an accident that caused a lot of sadness. Did you know? Nigeria is the most populous country in Africa! That means many, many people live there.

Who is Helping to Understand?

There's a special group of grown-ups called Amnesty International. They are like detectives who work to make sure everyone in the world is treated fairly and safely. They listen to people's stories and try to understand exactly what happened in Jilli village. They want to make sure that everyone learns from this sad event and that such accidents don't happen again. They are speaking up for the people of Jilli village. Did you know? Amnesty International works all over the world to help protect people's rights and make sure they are safe. They are like superheroes for fairness!

Life in a Village

Jilli is a village in Nigeria. What is a village like? Well, imagine a small, friendly town where almost everyone knows each other! People often live close to their families, and they help each other out. Villages are often surrounded by beautiful nature, maybe farms where people grow food. In villages, the market is a very important place because it's where people come together, not just to buy things, but also to share news and be part of their community. Did you know? Many people in villages grow their own food, like maize, millet, and beans, right in their gardens!

Why Markets are Special

Markets in Africa are not just places to shop. They are lively, colourful, and noisy places! You can find everything there, from fresh fruits and vegetables to beautiful fabrics, handmade crafts, and even farm animals. They are a big part of the culture and how people connect with each other. It's where the heart of the community often beats. That's why it's extra sad when something bad happens in such an important place. Did you know? Some markets in Africa have been around for hundreds of years, passed down through generations!

What Can We Do?

When sad things happen, it's important to remember that we can all wish for peace and safety for everyone. We can think about the children and families in Jilli village and hope that they can heal and feel safe again very soon. People are working hard to help them. Our hearts go out to everyone in Jilli village. Did you know? Even small acts of kindness, like sharing a toy or saying a nice word, can help make the world a happier place for everyone.

Looking Forward with Hope

Even when sad things happen, we must always remember to hope for a better tomorrow. We hope that the grown-ups who protect us will learn from this and work even harder to keep everyone safe. We also hope that the people in Jilli village will get all the help and comfort they need to feel better. Let's remember to be kind to each other and always wish for peace and safety for all children and families, everywhere in the world. Thank you for listening. Keep shining brightly!
summary: Many people were hurt and sadly lost their lives when an accidental air strike happened at a busy market in Jilli village, Nigeria.
whyMatters: This matters because we want all children and families in Africa and around the world to be safe, happy, and able to live peacefully in their homes and communities.
